anchor.description: Mascot centered on canvas. Text in a clean horizontal line separated by negative space. 3:4 portrait canvas. Transparent background.

crop.description: How much of the animal is visible in the frame.

options.description: Extreme crop to the face only. Forehead to chin fills the frame. Best for animals with famous internet nicknames or highly expressive faces.
Head and shoulders/upper chest visible. Tight crop, no full body. Best for composed postures and direct address.
Entire animal visible from head to tail/feet. Best for collapsed poses, side profiles, action poses, or when the body language IS the joke.

text_placement.description: Where the phrase sits relative to the mascot.

options.description: Text in a clean horizontal line below the mascot. Clean negative space separates them. The standard — keeps focus on the animal.
Text is a massive arched banner in the upper portion of the canvas. Mascot sits in the lower portion. Best for boastful or declarative phrases that need visual weight.
Short text above the mascot (2-4 words), mascot in the middle, main text below. Best for plea/reframe structures where the animal is caught between two statements.
options.canvas_split: text upper ~15%, mascot middle ~50%, text lower ~20%

options.description: Text integrated into the open space around a dynamic action pose. No fixed text position — it fills the gaps.
options.canvas_split: dynamic — text fills voids around diagonal pose

options.description: Text is small and grounded at the very bottom of the canvas. Mascot dominates the upper 80%. Best when the animal IS the joke and the text is just a label.
Text arches above and below the head, framing the face. Best for symmetrical close-up face compositions.
options.canvas_split: text arches upper ~20%, face middle ~50%, text lower ~15%

viewpoint.description: The camera angle relative to the animal.

options.description: Animal faces the viewer directly. Eye contact. Confrontational or inviting. Best for composed, direct, or pleading poses.
Clean side view of the full body. Animal is in profile. Best for weird/niche animals, deadpan labels, or showing body shape. Only one eye is visible. Only the near-side limbs are fully visible.
Animal is above the viewer, looking down. Mascot dominates the upper canvas. Best for obsessive or looming energy. Typically shows the underside of the chin/jaw and the top of the head/ears.
Animal is in mid-motion on a diagonal axis. Running, flying, lunging. HIGH ANATOMY RISK — requires explicit limb separation in the prompt. Wings/limbs must be fully extended and frozen in mid-action.

pose_energy.description: Free-text comma-separated energy descriptors for what the animal is physically doing. Use one or multiple. Most real postures blend 2+ energies — document them honestly rather than force-fitting into one bucket.

descriptor_vocabulary.description: Upright, structural dignity. Sitting or standing with posture. Eye contact possible.
Boneless, zero structural integrity. Lying flat, slumped, melting in place. No upright support.
Active paws or limbs reaching, pressing together, or held up expressively. Animal communicating through limb position.
descriptor_vocabulary.anatomy_risk: high — paws need explicit chunky-shape constraints in the prompt

descriptor_vocabulary.description: Mid-motion, dynamic. Running, flying, lunging, pouncing. Diagonal energy.
descriptor_vocabulary.anatomy_risk: high — limbs must be frozen and explicitly separated from body

descriptor_vocabulary.description: Looking down from above. Animal is above the viewer. Chin/underside visible.

pose_energy.dependency_note: If your pose_energy includes 'action' or 'gesturing' or if the pose description involves active limbs, anatomy overrides are REQUIRED regardless of which other energies are present. If your pose_energy includes 'collapsed', full_body crop is STRONGLY suggested. If your pose_energy includes 'peering', peering_down viewpoint is STRONGLY suggested.

invalid_combinations.reason: A face-only crop in side profile shows only the side of the head — no expression, no eye contact, no character.
invalid_combinations.fix: Use crop=full_body with viewpoint=side_profile, or crop=face_only with viewpoint=front_centered.

invalid_combinations.reason: A diagonal action pose with just a face floating on the canvas reads as a crop error — there's no body to show the action.
A collapsed/boneless pose has no meaning when only the face is visible — the body language IS the joke.
Gesturing requires visible paws/limbs. A face-only crop hides the gesture.
invalid_combinations.fix: Use crop=head_shoulders or full_body with pose_energy=gesturing.

invalid_combinations.reason: Action requires a visible body. A face-only crop eliminates the action context.
invalid_combinations.fix: Use crop=full_body with viewpoint=action_diagonal and pose_energy=action.

invalid_combinations.reason: Arches of text around a face don't work when the face is a small fraction of the canvas — the text arcs would overlap the body.
invalid_combinations.fix: Use crop=face_only with text_placement=arches_face, or use a different text placement.

invalid_combinations.reason: Arches of text around a head+shoulders crop will arc through empty space where the body should be, creating a visual gap.
A face-only crop has no negative space around the body for text to fill.
A tight crop leaves minimal negative space around the subject — not enough area for flowing text.
In side profile, only one front limb is fully visible. A gesturing pose (both paws pressed together, reaching out) requires both limbs visible from the front.

When the animal is peering down from above occupying the upper 80% of the canvas, text placed 'below' would sit well below the canvas center, creating too much negative space. Use text_placement=small_bottom instead.
A diagonal action pose's body is already occupying the lower canvas. Text placed below would overlap or be forced off the canvas edge.

counted_limbs_by_pose.collapsed_on_belly_front: Exactly 2 front legs visible splayed to sides. Back legs are HIDDEN under the body — do NOT describe them as separate or visible. Describing 4 separate limbs when the animal is belly-down forces the AI to grow extra limbs through the body.
counted_limbs_by_pose.collapsed_on_belly_side: Exactly 2 front legs + both back legs visible (side view). All 4 limbs splayed. Each leg separated from the body with visible negative space gaps.
counted_limbs_by_pose.collapsed_on_back: Exactly 4 limbs visible splayed upward. Belly is the focal point. Back legs are ABOVE the body in the air, front legs are above the head. Each leg separated — no merged anatomy.
counted_limbs_by_pose.collapsed_on_side: Exactly 3-4 limbs visible depending on angle. Near-side front and back legs fully visible, far-side limbs partially or fully visible. Each clearly separated.
counted_limbs_by_pose.standing_front: Exactly 4 legs visible. Front two and back two. Head above body. Tail visible if applicable.
counted_limbs_by_pose.standing_side: Exactly 4 legs visible in profile. Near-side legs fully visible, far-side legs partially visible behind near-side. Do NOT describe far-side legs as separate if they would naturally be hidden.
counted_limbs_by_pose.sitting_front: Exactly 2 front legs visible. Back legs are TUCKED under the body — NOT visible as separate limbs. Describing 4 limbs when the animal is sitting forces the AI to show back legs emerging unnaturally from the sides.
counted_limbs_by_pose.sitting_side: Both front legs visible. One or both back legs visible depending on angle. Far-side back leg may be hidden behind the body — do NOT require it to be visible.
counted_limbs_by_pose.flying_diagonal: Wings fully extended, frozen in mid-flap. Each wing clearly separated from the body. Back legs trailing. Front legs/feet tucked. NO merging between wings and body.

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: When an animal lies on its belly, the back legs are UNDER the body, not visible. Asking for all 4 limbs while the animal is on its belly forces the AI to add a 5th limb or grow back legs out of the belly.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Specify: 'Exactly 2 front legs visible splayed out to the sides. Back legs are tucked under the belly and NOT visible as separate limbs.'

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: When sitting, back legs fold under the body. They are not separately visible limbs. The AI either adds legs to the sides of the body or creates melting anatomy.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Specify: 'Exactly 2 front legs visible. Back legs folded under the body, not separately visible.'

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: In a true side profile, only one eye is visible. Asking for both eyes forces a 3/4 turn, which is not a side profile.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Specify: 'A clean side profile. Exactly one eye visible, the far eye is hidden behind the head.'

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: When peering down from above, the animal's face is angled downward. The eyes are looking down, not at the viewer. Direct eye contact is physically impossible from this angle.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Specify: 'Eyes looking downward past the viewer. No direct eye contact with the camera.'

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: A full-body crop by definition shows the entire animal. The face is a small portion of the canvas. Asking the face to dominate contradicts the crop.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Pick one: either crop=face_only (face dominates) or crop=full_body (whole animal visible, face is proportionally small).

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: Saying 'Background: TRANSPARENT' but also specifying a solid background color confuses the AI, which may render a colored background behind a transparent cutout.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: If background is TRANSPARENT, do NOT specify any background color. If a colored background is desired, change to a solid color and remove 'TRANSPARENT'.

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: A text frame border surrounds the animal on all 4 sides. Adding separate text below creates two text elements, which exceeds the max-2-elements rule and fights for visual weight.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Either use a text frame border (no separate text element) OR place text below (no frame border). Do not combine both.

common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: The prompt says 'NO PROPS' then describes an accessory (tie, hat, glasses). An accessory IS a prop. This contradiction confuses the AI.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: If the animal wears/carries an object, it is a prop. Remove the 'NO PROPS' statement or remove the prop description.

common_ai_execution_traps.trap: Exact limb count + pose that hides limbs
common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: Demanding 'exactly 4 limbs visible' in a pose that naturally hides 2 of them forces the AI to hallucinate extra body parts. The AI will insert limbs where they do not belong rather than fail the 'exactly' constraint.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Match limb counts to the reality of the pose. If only 2 front legs are visible, say 'exactly 2 front legs visible'. Do not assert counts for body parts the pose cannot show.

common_ai_execution_traps.trap: 'All 4 limbs splayed in different directions' for any pose
common_ai_execution_traps.why_it_fails: Vague directionality. The AI picks random limb angles, often creating asymmetrical spaghetti anatomy. Every limb needs a specific spatial relationship to the body and other limbs.
common_ai_execution_traps.fix: Describe each limb's position relative to the body: 'Front left leg splayed out to the left at 45 degrees. Front right leg splayed to the right at 30 degrees. Back legs [specific position].'

high_anatomy_risk.required_override: If active paws/limbs are visible: 'Thick, simple, chunky paws pressed together, NO individual fingers. Each limb clearly separated from the body with visible negative space gaps.' If mid-motion: 'Wings/limbs fully extended and frozen in mid-action, clearly separated from body. No merging.'

safety_rules.text_isolation: Text must never overlap or touch the mascot. Minimum 15% negative space buffer between mascot and text boundary.
safety_rules.max_elements: Maximum 2 visual elements (mascot + text). No decorative flourishes or extra elements.
safety_rules.no_3d_text: Flat 2D lettering only. No 3D extrusion, drop shadows, isometric text, or gradients.
safety_rules.background: TRANSPARENT. No solid backgrounds, no gradient backgrounds.

hard.description: Breaking these causes real failures (cost, platform rejection, AI hallucinations). Never override.
rules: canvas_ratio: 3:4 portrait — POD platforms reject non-standard ratios
text_isolation: minimum 15% negative space between text and mascot — touching text prints as mud at small sizes
flat_2d_lettering: no 3D text, no drop shadows, no extrusion — AI renders 3D text illegibly every time
transparent_background: POD requirement — colored backgrounds break mockup pipelines
limb_count_matches_pose: writing 'all 4 limbs' for a belly-down pose guarantees a 5th leg
limb_separation_for_high_risk: action_diagonal and gesturing poses require explicit limb separation — the AI WILL merge body and motion limbs otherwise
no_trademarked_phrases: legal risk, DMCA takedowns
max_3_colors_plus_outline: POD screen print cost — each additional color doubles production cost
hard.enforcement: Agent 3 MUST reject prompts that violate hard constraints. No exceptions.

anchor.description: Best practices. Deviate only when the design context demands it AND you document why. The system default is the anchor — deviating requires active justification.
rules: pose_energy_start: start thinking from what the animal is doing, then pick viewpoint, crop, text_placement — most designs benefit from this dependency order, but if a concept arrives as a specific crop (face-only of an expression), starting there is fine
single_posture_register: most real postures blend 2+ energies — document multiple descriptors if honest, do NOT force-fit one label
single_phrase_structure_label: novel combinations that don't match a template should be labeled 'custom synthesis' instead of force-labeled to a structure
word_count_under_8: a 10-word phrase that reads cleanly on a shirt is fine — the rule exists to prevent wall-of-text, not to enforce an exact count
font_matches_register: intentional contrast (monospace for a chaotic phrase) is a valid creative choice
expression_matches_phrase_energy: intentional contrast (tired expression + violent phrase) is the most popular 2026 pattern
palette_from_6_presets: blending palettes or inventing a custom one is fine if colors harmonize
pose_references_posture_registers: use reference/postures.json for vocabulary, not as a pick-one menu
anchor.enforcement: Agent 3 FLAGS violations and requires justification. Does NOT reject automatically — a reasoned override passes.

soft.description: Guidelines that exist as helpful defaults. Freely breakable when context supports it.
rules: single_paradox_type: animals feel complex emotions — a single label is reductive, use multiple descriptors if honest
proven_structures_from_19_templates: templates are starting points, not cages — custom structures that emerge from research often outperform templates
low_search_results_means_obscure: obscurity is a data point, not a failure — blue ocean is valuable
high_search_results_means_saturated: saturation is a factor, not a hard fail — a unique angle within a saturated niche still sells
tags_must_include_animal: register-only tags often convert better than animal-inclusive tags
soft.enforcement: Agent 3 notes deviations but does not flag them. They become data for Agent 6's retrospective.

decision_framework.step_1: READ reference/postures.json. REFERENCE ONLY — do not pick a pose yet. Just understand what postures exist.
decision_framework.step_2: Identify the core emotional register from Agent 1's research. This governs everything.
decision_framework.step_3: Pick pose_energy FIRST. This is the anchor decision — is the animal collapsing, gesturing, composed, in motion, or peering down? The pose is the physical manifestation of the emotional register. An exhausted register gets collapsed energy. A smug register gets composed energy. A chaotic register gets action energy.
decision_framework.step_4: Pick the viewpoint that serves the pose. A collapsed animal on the floor works from front_centered or side_profile. An action pose demands action_diagonal. A peering pose demands peering_down.
decision_framework.step_5: Pick the crop that fits the viewpoint. side_profile requires full_body. face_only only works with front_centered. head_shoulders works with front_centered or peering_down.
decision_framework.step_6: Pick the text placement that fits the remaining canvas space. If the animal is in the upper canvas (peering_down), text must be small_bottom. If action fills the canvas diagonally, text goes in negative_space. By default, text goes below.
decision_framework.step_7: Check your composition against the invalid_combinations table. If any combination appears there, change one of your choices.
decision_framework.step_8: Check anatomy_logistics.counted_limbs_by_pose for your chosen pose. Ensure the limb count in the prompt matches what the pose can actually show. If the pose hides limbs, do NOT demand them visible.
decision_framework.step_9: Check anatomy_logistics.common_ai_execution_traps for your chosen layout. If any trap applies, rewrite the prompt to avoid it.
decision_framework.step_10: Document your composition as: crop=__, text=__, view=__, pose=__.
